description: Interactive drag-and-sort activity for NCERT Grade 6 Science, Chapter 6 (Materials Around Us), Activity 6.6. Students drag six labelled object tiles into three bins — Transparent, Translucent, or Opaque. Correct drops lock in green with a one-line reason; wrong drops bounce back with a contextual hint. When all six are placed correctly, Table 6.4 renders at the bottom with the three columns filled. A reset button replays the activity. Responsive: drag-and-drop on desktop, tap-to-select-then-tap-bin on mobile.

chapter.subject: Science
chapter.section: 6.3.3 Explore materials through which one can see or cannot see

width.description: Maximum container width in pixels. The tool is fully responsive and will shrink below this width.

height.description: Height is content-driven by default; the layout adapts to viewport.

themeColor.description: Primary accent (purple from design system). Used in headers, progress, and primary bins.

darkMode.description: Reserved for future dark theme. Currently the tool renders in the light Singularity theme.

additionalProps.description: Tool-specific configuration. All fields are optional — sensible Activity 6.6 defaults are used.

items.description: Override the six default objects. Each item is the full sorter tile config.

name.description: Display label on the tile, e.g. 'Glass tumbler'

category.description: The correct bin this item belongs in

emoji.description: Single emoji shown on the tile, e.g. '🥃'

reason.description: One-line explanation shown when correctly placed. Keep under 80 characters.

hint.description: Short hint shown when student drops the item on the wrong bin. Should nudge thinking without revealing the answer.

categories.description: Override labels or descriptions of the three bins. Keys must be 'transparent', 'translucent', 'opaque'.

showTableOnComplete.description: When true (default), Table 6.4 with all three columns filled is rendered at the bottom once every item is correctly placed.

successMessage.description: Message shown in the success banner and table subtitle on completion.

examples.description: Default — exact Activity 6.6 from the NCERT textbook
Custom item set with everyday objects

items.name: Plastic bottle
items.reason: Clear plastic — you can see what is inside.
items.hint: Can you see the water level inside it clearly?

items.name: Tracing paper
items.reason: Hazy — light passes but shapes are blurred.
items.hint: Light gets through, but a clear view does not.

items.name: Metal spoon
items.hint: Shine a torch behind it — does any light come through?

examples.description: Show without table at the end (for a quick warm-up)

value.successMessage: Great work — you sorted them all!

defaultItems.name: Glass tumbler
defaultItems.reason: Clear glass — you can see straight through it.
defaultItems.hint: Hold a glass up — can you read the words behind it clearly?

defaultItems.name: Butter paper
defaultItems.reason: Light passes through, but the view is blurry — not clear.
defaultItems.hint: Light gets through, but shapes look hazy. Not fully clear, not fully blocked.

defaultItems.name: Eraser
defaultItems.reason: No light passes through — completely blocks the view.
defaultItems.hint: Try shining a torch behind it. Does any light come through?

defaultItems.name: Frosted glass
defaultItems.reason: Blurry — light passes but no clear view.
defaultItems.hint: Light gets through, but you only see fuzzy outlines.

defaultItems.name: Wooden board
defaultItems.reason: Wood blocks all light — you cannot see through it.
defaultItems.hint: Wood is dense — light has no way through.

defaultItems.name: Window glass
defaultItems.reason: A clear window — you see the outside view clearly.
defaultItems.hint: Think of a clean window pane. Can you see the garden through it?

interactions.desktop: HTML5 native drag-and-drop. Drag a tile, drop on a bin.
interactions.mobile: Tap-to-select then tap-to-place. Tap a tile to highlight it, then tap a bin to drop it there. Triggered automatically when viewport width < 760px.
interactions.correctDrop: Tile flies into the bin, locks with green check + accent border + one-line reason. Glow pulse animation.
interactions.wrongDrop: Bin briefly flashes red, the tile shakes and stays in the tray, and a purple hint bubble appears under the tile for ~3 seconds.
interactions.completion: All six placed → success banner appears, confetti animation plays, Table 6.4 renders at the bottom with all three columns filled. Each row staggers in.
interactions.reset: Clears all placements and hints; tray refills with all six tiles in order.

designSystem.shape: Rounded pill buttons (24px horizontal padding), 14–18px cards, 999px badges

responsive.below: Single-column bins, 2-column tray, tap-to-place input mode
responsive.above: Three-column bins, auto-fit tray (~140px tiles), drag-and-drop input mode

accessibility.keyboard: Native drag-and-drop on desktop; tap-to-place on mobile (works with assistive tech taps).
accessibility.fontScaling: Uses relative px sizing consistent with 16px root.
accessibility.color: All bin colours have white text with WCAG-AA-grade contrast; success and error states use icons + text, not colour alone.
accessibility.motion: Animations are short (<1s) and non-essential — completion still works without them. Confetti is decorative and pointer-events-none.

agentNotes.whenToUse: Use this tool when a Grade 6 student is studying section 6.3.3 of the Curiosity textbook (transparent / translucent / opaque materials) or doing Activity 6.6. Also use as a quick formative-assessment activity any time the concepts come up in conversation.
agentNotes.doNotUseWhen: The student needs a long explanation (use prose instead). The student is on a topic other than light-transmission classification (use a different sorter or build a new one).
agentNotes.instructionLine: Drag each of the six objects into Transparent, Translucent, or Opaque. Read the reason on each correct drop.
